Title: HY5 integrates light and electrical signaling to trigger a jasmonate burst for nematode defense in tomato

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red light; supplementary far-red light; root-knot nematode infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates RKN-induced HY5 accumulation, JA biosynthesis and nematode resistance from the shoot
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Photoreceptor mutant comparison; reciprocal grafting; JA quantification; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phyB is necessary for RKN-induced JA/JA-Ile accumulation and expression of LOXF, AOS and AOC. 2. Shoot phyB, rather than root phyB, determines systemic resistance to RKN. 3. phyB is required for RKN-induced HY5 accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating red/far-red light responses or phyB signaling could improve inducible nematode defense.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad developmental/light-quality effects not evaluated.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: fatty acid hydroperoxide JA precursor intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates α-linolenic acid in the early octadecanoid pathway toward jasmonate formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes RKN-induced JA/JA-Ile accumulation and nematode resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis of HY5/cam2 backgrounds affecting LOXF expression; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves at 1 dpi after RKN infection; activation enhanced under red light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-knot nematode infection; red light; supplementary far-red light; darkness; ruthenium red
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOXF transcript is induced in leaves after RKN infection in a phyB-HY5-CaM2 dependent manner. 2. HY5 directly binds and activates the LOXF promoter. 3. Increased LOXF expression is associated with higher JA/JA-Ile accumulation and greater nematode resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be co-targeted with HY5-mediated light/electrical signaling to strengthen JA-dependent nematode defense.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as a direct HY5 target and JA biosynthetic marker gene in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxide to an unstable allene oxide intermediate in jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes RKN-induced JA/JA-Ile accumulation and nematode resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in mutants/grafts; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves at 1 dpi after RKN infection; activation enhanced under red light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-knot nematode infection; red light; supplementary far-red light; darkness; ruthenium red
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS expression is induced by RKN in leaves under phyB/HY5/CaM2 control. 2. HY5 directly binds and activates the AOS promoter. 3. AOS induction correlates with enhanced JA burst and nematode resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stackable JA biosynthetic target for improving nematode resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct HY5 target in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes the allene oxide intermediate to form OPDA, a jasmonate precursor essential for resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes RKN-induced JA/JA-Ile accumulation and nematode resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in mutants/grafts; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves at 1 dpi after RKN infection; activation enhanced under red light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-knot nematode infection; red light; supplementary far-red light; darkness; ruthenium red
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC transcript accumulation after RKN infection requires phyB, HY5 and CaM2. 2. HY5 directly binds and activates the AOC promoter. 3. AOC induction is linked to stronger JA burst and nematode resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway target to boost inducible jasmonate production under pest attack.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct HY5 target in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-knot nematode infection; red light; supplementary far-red light; darkness
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phyB-mediated red light signaling; GLR3.5-dependent electrical/Ca2+ signaling; CaM2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: jasmonate biosynthesis; systemic electrical signaling reinforcement
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates LOXF, AOS, AOC, HY5 and GLR3.5
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedforward/autoregulatory loop via self-activation and reinforcement of GLR3.5-dependent electrical signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional complex with CaM2 to enhance DNA binding/transcriptional activity
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CaM2; phyB (genetic upstream relationship); GLR3.5 (transcriptional target)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA burst and increases nematode resistance; mobile HY5 from shoot to root sustains systemic electrical signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central hub integrating light and electrical signaling to trigger and amplify JA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutants; overexpression; reciprocal grafting; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; immunoblotting; electrical potential recording
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Accumulated in leaves after RKN infection in a phyB- and GLR3.5/CaM2-dependent manner; detected in roots via shoot-to-root movement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is required in shoots for RKN-induced JA biosynthesis and resistance. 2. HY5 directly activates JA biosynthetic genes LOXF, AOS and AOC and also activates its own promoter. 3. HY5 protein moves from shoot to root and activates GLR3.5 to maintain systemic electrical signaling. 4. CaM2 interaction enhances HY5 DNA binding and transcriptional output, amplifying the JA burst.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulatory target for engineering inducible nematode resistance through enhanced JA biosynthesis and systemic signaling, but deployment should consider broad pleiotropic roles in light/developmental pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects on photomorphogenesis, root growth, nutrient uptake and hormone crosstalk were not evaluated here.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RKN-induced HY5 accumulation is specifically compromised in phyB mutants; HY5 variants with impaired CaM-binding show weaker defense activation.

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (interaction with HY5 shown in nucleus)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-knot nematode infection; Ca2+ signaling; red light
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GLR3.5-dependent electrical/Ca2+ signals; phyB-dependent light signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Activation of HY5-dependent jasmonate biosynthetic transcription
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HY5 protein activity; indirectly LOXF; AOS; AOC; HY5; GLR3.5
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated enhancement of DNA binding/transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances HY5 activation of LOXF, AOS, AOC, HY5 and GLR3.5
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in amplification loop through HY5 self-activation and GLR3.5 reinforcement
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA burst and nematode resistance by potentiating HY5 activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential amplifier linking electrical/Ca2+ signaling to transcriptional induction of JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutants; split-LUC; BiFC; pull-down; Co-IP; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most highly expressed CaM gene in leaves; induced by RKN; induction reduced in glr3.5 and phyB mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaM2 is the predominant CaM transcript in leaves and is induced by RKN in a GLR3.5/phyB-dependent manner. 2. Loss of CaM2 reduces HY5 accumulation, JA biosynthetic gene expression, JA/JA-Ile accumulation and resistance. 3. CaM2 directly interacts with HY5 in a Ca2+-enhanced manner. 4. CaM2 boosts HY5 DNA binding and transcriptional activation, amplifying jasmonate biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for strengthening inducible defense signaling, especially in combination with HY5, though broad Ca2+ signaling functions warrant caution.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to general Ca2+ signaling roles; not assessed in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutations in HY5 CaM-binding domain weaken CaM2 interaction and reduce defense outputs.

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-knot nematode infection; light/dark conditions; red light
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: HY5 transcriptional activation; root-knot nematode attack
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Systemic electrical/Ca2+ signal propagation leading to JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CaM2 and HY5 accumulation/function indirectly via electrical signaling cascade
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Electrical/Ca2+ signal propagation; channel-mediated activation of downstream signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes CaM2 transcript/protein accumulation and HY5 accumulation indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive loop because HY5 activates GLR3.5, and GLR3.5-dependent signaling promotes HY5/CaM2 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for systemic electrical signaling, JA burst and nematode resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key electrical-signaling node that enables and reinforces systemic induction of jasmonate biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; transcript/protein measurements; electrical potential recording; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by RKN in roots, stems and leaves; activation attenuated in hy5, cam2 and hy5 cam2 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLR3.5-dependent electrical signaling is required for RKN-induced HY5 and CaM2 accumulation. 2. HY5 directly binds and activates the GLR3.5 promoter. 3. This creates a positive systemic loop reinforcing root-to-shoot electrical signaling and jasmonate burst.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance long-distance inducible defense signaling in tomato, potentially in combination with HY5.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible broad effects on electrical/Ca2+ signaling and growth not evaluated.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a TF, GLR3.5 is a core signaling gene directly linked to metabolite induction and systemic defense.
